compiled_state.title: As a DSO user, I can create tickets with tags at creation time
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add --tags flag to ticket create command and fix the ticket reducer to read tags from CREATE events.
**Why**: This is the foundational plumbing that enables all downstream tag-based routing. Without atomic tag creation, the CLI_user tag cannot be set when a bug is filed.
**Scope**:
- IN: --tags flag parsing in ticket-create.sh, tags inclusion in CREATE event JSON, reducer CREATE handler reading data.get('tags', [])
- OUT: Tag-based routing logic (handled by story 2), agent guidance for when to apply tags (handled by story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running ticket create bug "test" --tags CLI_user creates a ticket whose ticket show output includes CLI_user in the tags array
  ← Satisfies: "ticket create supports a --tags flag that sets tags atomically at creation time"
- When this story is complete, running ticket create bug "test" without --tags creates a ticket with an empty tags array (backward compatible)
  ← Satisfies: "ticket create supports a --tags flag that sets tags atomically at creation time"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Reducer CREATE handler change must stay consistent with EDIT handler tag parsing pattern (comma-separated string → list conversion)
